criteria: The agent has read Section 4 (Method) completely to understand the core IFT methodology, including Intuitive Preference Estimation and Dynamic Relation Propagation.
The agent has read Algorithm 1 carefully to understand the three-step IFT process: Inference One Step Ahead, Intuitive Preference Estimation, and Dynamic Relation Propagation.
The agent has read Section 5.1 completely to understand the experimental settings, including datasets, models, scenarios, baselines, and benchmarks.

The agent has read Appendix B.1 to understand the NLP hyperparameter settings (epoch=3, mini batch size=8, gradient accumulation step=64, warmup ratio=0.1, cosine scheduler, lr=5e-7, RMSprop optimizer, bfloat16 precision, λ=0.2, decay factor=0.95).

The agent has read Section 5.3 and Appendix B.2 to understand the Frozen Lake experiment setup, including the two-layer fully connected neural network, optimal/sub-optimal trajectories, and MSE evaluation metric.
The plan includes loading UltraChat-200k as the single-target dataset and UltraFeedback-60k as the pairwise preference dataset.

The plan includes implementing the three-step IFT algorithm: (1) inference one step ahead to generate model predictions, (2) fusing embeddings with λ, and (3) computing the cumulative-sum weighted loss.
The plan includes implementing all baseline methods: SFT, DPO, TDPO, ORPO, and SimPO, with their respective hyperparameters (β=0.1 for DPO/TDPO, β=0.25 for ORPO, β=2.0/γ/β=0.8 for SimPO).
The plan includes two training scenarios: (1) direct alignment from Mistral-7B-v0.1 using UltraFeedback-60k, and (2) sequential SFT+PO starting from Mistral-7B-sft-beta with UltraFeedback-60k.
The plan includes evaluating on Open-LLM Leaderboard benchmarks (ARC-Challenge 25-shot, MMLU 5-shot, TruthfulQA 0-shot, WinoGrande 5-shot, GSM8K 5-shot) using chat template.
The plan includes evaluating on LLM-based benchmarks: Alpaca-Eval, Alpaca-Eval-2 (win-rate and lc win-rate), and TL;DR win-rate judged by GPT-4.

The plan includes the Frozen Lake experiment using a two-layer fully connected neural network, with optimal and sub-optimal trajectories, evaluating by MSE distance between trained and optimal transition matrices.

The code implements Algorithm 1 Step 1 (Inference One Step Ahead): for each token position t in [1,N], predict the distribution P'_t = π_θ(s*[0:t-1]) and sample s^θ[t] = argmax P'_t using the ground truth prefix.

The code implements the disturbance function δ_θ as a fused embedding: e = (1-λ)E(s*) + λE(s^θ), where E is the embedding layer, combining ground truth and model-generated token embeddings with mixing coefficient λ.

The code implements Algorithm 1 Step 2 (Intuitive Preference Estimation): for each token t, compute P''_t = (π_θ/E)(e[0:t-1]) using the fused embedding as input to the model (with the embedding layer bypassed), and compute per-token cross-entropy loss L_t.

The code implements Algorithm 1 Step 3 (Dynamic Relation Propagation): for each token t, compute the cumulative-sum weight w_t = sum_{i=t}^{N} α^{N-t} * L_i, and compute the final IFT loss as L_IFT = w · L (dot product of weights and per-token losses).
The IFT loss function L_IFT is implemented as the double-sum formula: E[-sum_{n=0}^{N} sum_{i=n}^{N} log T_θ(a*_i, δ_θ(s*_i))], which satisfies the Bellman Equation per state.
The code uses λ=0.2 for the mixing coefficient in the disturbance function and a decay factor of 0.95 in the cumulative-sum weighting (Dynamic Relation Propagation).

The training uses the specified hyperparameters: epoch=3, mini batch size=8, gradient accumulation step=64, warmup ratio=0.1, cosine scheduler, learning rate=5e-7, RMSprop optimizer, bfloat16 precision.
The code loads and uses Mistral-7B-v0.1 as the base model for the direct PO scenario and Mistral-7B-sft-beta (pre-fine-tuned on UltraChat-200k) for the sequential SFT+PO scenario.
The code implements the SFT loss (standard cross-entropy on ground truth tokens) as a baseline, applied to Mistral-7B-v0.1 with UltraFeedback-60k (60k randomly sampled targets).
The code implements the DPO loss with β=0.1 and a reference model, using pairwise positive/negative data from UltraFeedback-60k.
The code implements ORPO (β=0.25, no reference model) and SimPO (β=2.0, γ/β=0.8, no reference model) as baselines with their respective hyperparameters.

The code balances data volume between IFT/SFT and pairwise methods in scenario 1: IFT and SFT use 60k randomly sampled UltraChat targets plus 60k UltraFeedback chosen responses (120k total), while DPO/TDPO/ORPO/SimPO use 120k (60k pairs × 2) from UltraFeedback.

The code implements the Frozen Lake experiment: a two-layer fully connected neural network policy, an environment with one optimal and one sub-optimal trajectory, and MSE distance evaluation between the trained policy's and the optimal policy's transition matrices.
For the Frozen Lake experiment, the optimal trajectory is used as target for SFT and IFT, while DPO and ORPO use the optimal trajectory as positive and sub-optimal as negative samples.
The code evaluates on Open-LLM Leaderboard tasks (ARC-Challenge, MMLU, TruthfulQA, WinoGrande, GSM8K) with the specified shot counts and chat template applied to all models.
The code evaluates on LLM-based benchmarks: Alpaca-Eval and Alpaca-Eval-2 (win-rate and lc win-rate), and TL;DR using GPT-4 as judge to measure win-rate against ground truth answers.
The code saves checkpoints every 20k steps and selects the checkpoint with the best average score across benchmarks for final reporting.
IFT training on Mistral-7B-v0.1 with UltraFeedback-60k (scenario 1) runs successfully without errors across all epochs.
All baseline methods (SFT, DPO, TDPO, ORPO, SimPO) training on Mistral-7B (and Mistral-7B-sft-beta for sequential scenario) execute successfully without errors.
The Open-LLM Leaderboard evaluation script runs successfully for all trained models and produces ARC, MMLU, TruthfulQA, WinoGrande, and GSM8K scores.
The Alpaca-Eval and TL;DR evaluation scripts run successfully for all trained models and produce win-rate results.
The Frozen Lake experiment runs successfully for all methods (SFT, DPO, ORPO, IFT) and produces MSE distance curves over fine-tuning steps.

The execution outcomes match Table 2: IFT achieves the highest average score (59.61) among all methods when fine-tuning with UltraFeedback-60k on the Open-LLM Leaderboard, outperforming SFT (58.65), DPO (58.28), ORPO (57.70), and SimPO (55.15).

The execution outcomes match Table 2: IFT excels particularly in TruthfulQA (57.65) and GSM8K (44.73) among single-policy methods fine-tuned with UltraFeedback-60k, but has a relatively larger gap vs DPO on ARC-Challenge and MMLU.

The execution outcomes match Table 3: IFT achieves the highest Alpaca-Eval win-rate (85.18) and TL;DR win-rate (92.63) among methods fine-tuned directly on UltraFeedback-60k, securing top-two rankings in nearly all LLM-based benchmark tasks.

The execution outcomes match Table 2 (sequential recipe): IFT achieves average score 58.22 with only 260k data, outperforming SFT+SFT (56.99), SFT+TDPO (57.12), SFT+ORPO (57.35), and SFT+SimPO (56.94), while using less data than DPO-based methods (320k).
The execution outcomes match Figure 3: IFT achieves a much lower MSE distance to the optimal policy than SFT and ORPO in the Frozen Lake experiment, but slightly higher (worse) than DPO.

The execution outcomes match Table 5: IFT achieves the highest average score on Open-LLM Leaderboard for both Gemma-2B (42.39) and LLaMA3-8B (60.92) when fine-tuned with the respective UltraFeedback variants.

The execution outcomes match Table 7: IFT with noisy lambda achieves 60.56 average on Open-LLM Leaderboard, higher than standard IFT (59.61), indicating a potential robustness enhancement to the λ coefficient.
